title: selaginella plumosa var. radicata
intro: Selaginella plumosa var. radicata, also known as Polypody, is a species of evergreen, creeping, and perennial plant native to Asia, belonging to the genus Selaginella, a type of spore-producing plant. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Selaginella plumosa var. radicata include its ability to improve respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
